page: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in memory bandwidth and compression tasks. It achieves a top-tier rating in single-threaded gzip compression at 34.25 GB/sec and Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2207.0. Large block uncached memory read and write speeds are strong, measuring 178.37 GB/sec and 83.61 GB/sec respectively. The processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) contributes to strong performance in specialized workloads, such as LLM text generation for small models (357.67 tokens/sec) and image processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.8xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU allocation, making it highly suitable for compute-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage. The instance is optimized for workloads such as high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and CPU-based machine learning inference. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider for database-heavy applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server delivers strong benchmark results, notably in single-threaded gzip compression (34.25 GB/sec), Geekbench single-core performance, and large-block memory bandwidth. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, enhancing cryptographic and vector processing capabilities. With a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, the c8i.8xlarge is designed for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-throughput web serving and CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and image processing
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, cryptography, and CPU-based inference
